pattern: Count numbers in a range that lack a special structure by recognising that special numbers are squares of primes.
hints: A number with exactly 2 proper divisors has exactly 3 total divisors — think about what form such numbers take.
Numbers with exactly 3 divisors are squares of primes (p^2), so collect all primes up to sqrt(r) with a sieve.
Count how many p^2 values fall in [l, r] and subtract from the total range length.

edge_inputs.description: Single element equal to a special number (4 = 2^2)
Range spanning exactly one special number
Range from 4 to 16 containing two specials (4 and 9)
Range starting just after a special number

stress.description: Random pairs (l, r) with l <= r <= 1e9
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    l = rng.randint(1, 10**9)
    r = rng.randint(l, min(l + 10**7, 10**9))
    return [l, r]
